title: Bridge Essay: Dopamine D2 receptors in addiction-like reward dysfunction and compulsive eating

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Dopamine D2 receptors in addiction-like reward dysfunction and compulsive eating in obese rats.** — Nat Neurosci. PMID: 20348917. DOI: 10.1038/nn.2519. We found that development of obesity was coupled with emergence of aprogressively worsening deficit in neural reward responses. Similar changes in reward homeostasis induced by cocaine or heroin are considered to be crucial in triggering the transition from casual to compulsive drug-taking. Accordingly, we detected compulsive-like feeding behavior in obese but not lean rats, measured as palatable food consumption that was resistant to disruption by an aversive conditioned stimulus.
